questions.question_text: Does the patient have an impairment or a disturbance in the functioning of the mind or brain?
If YES, what is the nature of impairment of or a disturbance in the functioning of the mind or brain?

answer_options: No, not likely to regain capacity
questions.question_text: Can the decision or treatment be delayed because the patient is likely to regain capacity in the future?
Does the patient understand the relevant information about the decision for their admission for assessment, care and treatment or discharge?
Is the patient able to retain the information/explanation long enough to make the decision about their admission for assessment, care and treatment, or discharge?
Is the patient able to weigh the information in the balance as part of the process of making the decision for their admission for care and treatment or discharge?
Is the patient able to communicate their decision either by speech, sign language or by any other means?
Does the patient have all of the relevant information to make the decision?
Would the patient be able to understand if the information was presented in a different way?
Would changing location make the patient feel more comfortable and help them to understand better?
Can anyone support the patient to make a decision or express their view such as a family member or carer?
Is there a family member/friend/LPA who can act in the patient's best interest? (If LPA in place, please clarify type)
Name, Relationship and Contact details of this person
LPA type - Financial or Health & Welfare

Conclusion: In my opinion, based on my own assessment and following consultation with appropriate others, the above-named has the capacity / lacks the capacity to consent to the following decision for care and treatment
